compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The enriched sidecars (stories A–C) and the completion attestation are reachable today only via
scattered internal helpers, and there is no single call that returns a ticket's FULL retained review
history or resolves its associated code reviews. The follow-on audit UI epic and any script need one
documented read surface.

## Dependency

`depends_on` stories A, B, C. A/B produce the enriched `*_v2` review sidecars this layer reads; C adds
`completion_sidecar.latest_pass_record` (the completion PASS record). Symbols added by those
`depends_on` tickets are treated as existing.

## What

A read layer that, for a ticket id, returns an `AuditTrail` with: (a) the full retained history of
plan-review sidecars; (b) the completion record; (c) the associated code reviews, resolved via the
ticket's inbound `relates_to` links from `code_review` tickets, each with its full sidecar history.
Exposed as a library function, a CLI subcommand, and a read-only MCP tool.

The completion field composes three DISJOINT sources (a completion result lives in different places by
outcome), so it is a struct, not a flat dict:

```python
class CompletionRecord(TypedDict):
    attestation: dict | None   # state['attestations']['completion-verifier'] from show_ticket (signed PASS close)
    pass_record: dict | None   # completion_sidecar.latest_pass_record (dependency C; PASS close)
    fail_record: dict | None   # completion_sidecar.latest_fail_verdict (blocked FAIL close)

class AuditTrail(TypedDict):
    ticket: dict                       # rebar.show_ticket(ticket_id)
    plan_reviews: list[dict]           # newest-first; [] if none
    completion: CompletionRecord       # any/all sub-fields may be None
    code_reviews: list[dict]           # each {artifact_ticket_id, history: list[dict]}; [] if no relates_to links
```

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` and `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — add
  `all_review_results(ticket_id, *, repo_root=None)` history enumerators (walk the ticket's
  `REVIEW_RESULT` events newest→oldest, schema-tolerant across v1/v2), complementing the existing
  `latest_*` readers.
- New `src/rebar/audit/read.py` — `audit_trail(ticket_id, *, repo_root=None) -> AuditTrail`. Composes:
  `rebar.show_ticket` for the ticket + its `attestations['completion-verifier']`; the plan-review
  history; the completion `latest_pass_record`/`latest_fail_verdict` from
  `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py`; and the code reviews by reading the ticket's inbound
  `relates_to` links (from `rebar.show_ticket`'s links) to `code_review`-type tickets, each via
  `all_review_results`.
- CLI: register `audit` as an INTERCEPT in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py::main` (mirroring the
  `workflow`/`llm`/`explain` intercepts — an arm `if argv and argv[0] == "audit": return
  _audit(argv[1:])` that owns its own `--help`; NO `help/*.txt` file and NO `known_subcommands` change,
  which is the intercept convention). `rebar audit show <ticket> [--output json]` lives under it.
- MCP: add a read-only `audit_trail` tool inside `register_read_tools` in `src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py`,
  decorated `@mcp.tool(annotations=_ANN["READ_ONLY"])` so it is available regardless of
  `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`.

## Approach rationale

A single aggregator over the existing readers + the existing `relates_to` links reuses the linkage the
reviewbot already writes (`emit_code_review_artifact` links each code-review artifact `relates_to` its
work ticket), so no new linking mechanism is introduced. The `audit` CLI intercept matches how other
composite/heavy subcommands (`workflow`, `llm`) register — owning their `--help` without a golden
help-text capture.

## Compatibility & rollback

Pure addition (new readers + new intercept subcommand + new read-only tool); no existing behavior
changes. Schema-tolerant readers accept v1 and v2. Reverting removes the new surface with no data
impact.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `audit_trail(ticket_id)` returns an `AuditTrail` whose keys are exactly `ticket`,
      `plan_reviews`, `completion` (with `attestation`/`pass_record`/`fail_record`), and
      `code_reviews`, newest-first, with `[]`/`None` for absent records. Oracle: an integration test
      seeding a ticket with multiple plan-review sidecars, a completion record, and a
      `relates_to`-linked `code_review` ticket with its own sidecar, then asserting the exact key set,
      nullability, and ordering.
- [ ] `rebar audit show <ticket> --output json` prints that structure. Oracle: a CLI subprocess test
      (test-design §3 CLI) asserting on the emitted JSON keys.
- [ ] The MCP `audit_trail` tool returns the structure and is read-only (available with
      `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1`). Oracle: a test invoking the registered tool under that env.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

file_impact.reason: completion pass/fail readers composed into trail

compiled_state.title: audit read layer (full-history readers + relates_to resolution + CLI/MCP)
